title: Agent Output Envelope
description: Structured JSON envelope every run-time agent emits alongside its human-readable markdown report. Build-time agents MAY use it but are not required to.

mode.description: One of the modes declared in the agent's frontmatter.modes.

run_id.description: Optional correlation id the caller assigns.

inputs_received.description: Echo of the inputs the agent used, after defaults are applied. Sensitive values (credentials) MUST be redacted.

summary.description: One paragraph — the deliverable in a single screen.

confidence_rationale.description: One-line reason for the score, keyed to the rubric in AGENT_CONTRACT.md.

findings.description: Structured deliverable findings. Optional — some agents emit pure documents via `deliverables[]` instead.

deliverables.description: Artifacts the agent produced (metadata XML stubs, markdown reports, proposed patches).

target_path.description: Where this deliverable is intended to land in the user's repo, if applicable.

process_observations.description: Structured Process Observations. Schema: schemas/observation.schema.json. Per AGENT_CONTRACT.md this block is REQUIRED; an empty array means the agent genuinely observed nothing beyond the deliverable.

refusal.description: Set iff the agent stopped per an Escalation/Refusal rule. When set, all other deliverable fields MAY be absent.

code.description: One of the codes in REFUSAL_CODES.md.

followups.description: Recommended follow-up agents. Each entry references a real agents/<id>/.
